Sky ='A Khati =6min
This is applicable only when day duration is 12 hours (30
Khati). When day span and night span changes, the time span
assigned to tatvas will also change proportionately. That is,
except for March 22 and September 21 on which the day and
night are equal, the span of tatvas given above will not match.
For all the other days, proportional change should be applied
to each tatva duration. All texts agree on the span that should
be applied to each tatva when day duration is 12 hours. For
example, Prasnamarga quote is given below-
F rrrawu
FIC!t CJ fRRlt ouSOJ1ilt!Fn(ar}~-
mswf ~ ~ EJfe<i>Nlc:Ji:wiwl:
[Earth= 30 min, Water= 24 min, Fire= 18 min, Air= 12
min, Sky = 6 min; is the duration that should be applied to
both odd and even yama]
Now as we have necessary basic understanding about
Y ama, Pancha tatva and Gulika let us move on to next section
on the application of these and other methods for birth time
rectification.

Birth Time Rectification
